This may have been the result of malware or the re-use of credentials previously exposed.
a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key

information from the compromised AWS server being posted on stolen data bazaar
46 million player usernames and SHA-1 hashed passwords were stolen and leaked

attack_chain.description: Attacker gained access to a WildWorks employee's Slack account using compromised user credentials (possibly via malware or credential reuse).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an unauthorized user gained access to the WildWorks' Slack workspace through compromised WildWorks user credentials

evidence_chunks.quote: an unauthorized user gained access to the WildWorks' Slack workspace through compromised WildWorks user credentials
an unauthorized user gained access to the WildWorks' Slack workspace through compromised WildWorks user credentials

attack_chain.description: Within the compromised Slack workspace, attackers discovered a private AWS key stored in a staff Slack channel.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key

evidence_chunks.quote: a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key
a staff Slack channel was compromised by malicious people who discovered a private AWS key

attack_chain.description: Attackers used the discovered AWS private key to authenticate to the vendor's AWS infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: We believe our vendor's server was compromised some time between October 10-12, 2020

attack_chain.description: Attackers accessed the user account database on the compromised AWS server and extracted ~46 million user records including usernames, SHA-1 hashed passwords, and ~12,000 parents' names and billing addresses.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The database theft most likely occurred in the same October 10-12, 2020 time window

evidence_chunks.quote: The database theft most likely occurred in the same October 10-12, 2020 time window
The database theft most likely occurred in the same October 10-12, 2020 time window

attack_chain.description: Stolen database was exfiltrated and later posted for sale on the RaidForums stolen data marketplace.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: information from the compromised AWS server being posted on stolen data bazaar raidforums[.]com

evidence_chunks.quote: information from the compromised AWS server being posted on stolen data bazaar raidforums[.]com
information from the compromised AWS server being posted on stolen data bazaar raidforums[.]com

initial_access_vector: Compromised WildWorks employee Slack credentials (possibly via malware or password reuse) allowed attacker into the Slack workspace, where a private AWS key was found in a staff channel.
persistence_mechanism: None reported; WildWorks stated all relevant systems were altered and secured after the intrusion.
privilege_escalation: Not required — discovered AWS private key provided direct access to the vendor's AWS server.
